statement.p: To estimate the health of the black-tailed deer population in the Jewell Meadow Wildlife Area, the Oregon Department of Fish and Wildlife caught, tagged, and released 98 black-tailed deer. A month later, they returned and observed 88 black-tailed deer, 11 of which had tags. Approximately how many black-tailed deer are in the Jewell Meadow Wildlife Area?

solution.p: Let x represent how many deer are in the Jewell Meadow Area. We set up a proportional equation:

p.me: \begin{aligned} \frac{\text{tagged deer}}{\text{total deer}} \amp= \frac{\text{tagged deer}}{\text{total deer}}\\ \frac{11}{88} \amp= \frac{98}{x}\\ \frac{11}{88} \multiplyright{88\cdot x} \amp= \frac{98}{x}\multiplyright{88\cdot x}\\ 11 \cdot x \amp= 98\cdot 88\\ \divideunder{11 \cdot x}{11} \amp= \divideunder{98\cdot 88}{11}\\ x \amp= 784 \end{aligned}

solution.p: There are approximately 784 deer in the Jewell Meadow Area.
